review_body_text: Overall culture and workplace environment is good. But they are using old system and required to login to so many system. Management also doesn’t have a clear and fix direction. Everything is keep changing every month.

review_title: Good place to start your career

A lot of parties or events, good for extrovert but not a good thing for introverts. Still a lot of manual work and very volume based KPI. Team lead quality is average. Good benefits like flexcare.

review_title: It's a great place to work... but the supervisors...
review_body_text: I worked here before and the work load is quite ok and bearable. Only 'some' supervisor are really getting on your nerve... overall, it's a good place to work
The benefit are superb , you can claim everything for personal things ie hotel , flight ticket , sport & etc. But for working environment, quiet tough. The pressure unbelievable.

review_title: While the job offered some valuable learning experiences, the overall environment at UOB Bank—particularly post-Citi Bank migration—was demoralizing.
review_body_text: A typical day was fast-paced, handling high volumes of customer issues with tight performance targets. I learned to manage pressure, improve my communication skills, and adapt quickly especially after the Citi Bank migration. Unfortunately, management was unresponsive and showed little concern for employee well-being. The culture was heavily politicized, with favoritism and internal politics often overshadowing merit and hard work. There was no work-life balance, and internal politics were overwhelming. Citi staff were treated poorly after the transition, which made things even harder. The hardest part was staying motivated in a toxic environment. The best part was helping customers and resolving their issues—it gave a real sense of accomplishment. Overall, not a supportive place to grow long-term.
No work life balance Pay is high Stressful working place Good benefit Politic environment Annual leave a lot Manager stress Long working hour Good place to learn

Poor management and leadership, with a lack of recognition for employees who go above and beyond their regular duties. During bonus and increment cycles, staff handling only BAU tasks often receive higher total compensation than those who take on additional responsibilities. Moreover, there is a lack of transparency in the compensation distribution process. Favoritism from both leadership and HRBP influences career growth, resulting in the same individuals receiving promotions every 2–3 years, while others are overlooked. Despite implementing 360-degree feedback and the Employee Engagement Survey (EES), employee concerns remain unaddressed. The work environment is highly political and toxic, with poor work-life balance. The recent acquisition of Citi’s business appears to have been poorly managed, leading to a hiring freeze post-merger. As a result, employees are now handling workloads far beyond their capacity, yet compensation remains minimal, with low increments and bonuses. While it is understandable that the bank must demonstrate profitability to shareholders, it is crucial to acknowledge the efforts of employees who drive this success.
Good company to work for. Great benefits and team. Sufficient AL provided. Unlimited health benefits. Flex care provided with more than 10 categories claimable
Colleagues are supportive and helpful but management is poor. Impose very strict and lousy rules and most of the time, very pushy and keep forcing staffs to perform OTs.
